topic: EXP-277 build-scripts patch package — phantom integration, factual error still live
our_argument_summary: EXP-277 is a build-scripts patch package (not a section rewrite). It bundles three patches to close five EXP-256 c1/c2 follow-up issues: P1 fixes 'August 12, 2026 annular solar eclipse' to 'total solar eclipse' at generate-html.js line 1754 (eg-falsifiability paragraph; the eclipse is total per NASA Besselian magnitude 1.0386 and our own Section 2.7 line 2949). P2 changes the verdict-bar aria-label noun from 'claims' to 'catalog entries' (template literal at line 489) so screen-reader element-nav matches the visible bar caption ('71 catalog entries = 69 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims'). P3 inserts five new test.js assertions guarding both fixes plus an exactly-once check on ds-falsifiability-module that EXP-271 omitted. The expansion-tracker, closed-issues, and 2026-04-29T09:23 daily-report all record EXP-277 as INTEGRATED with patches applied and 3506 tests passing.

proposal_review.proposal_diagnosis_notes: All five issues being addressed are real. The 'annular' wording at line 1754 is a clear factual error — Section 2.7 (line 2949 of docs/index.html) correctly states 'magnitude of 1.0386 — total, not annular' citing NASA Besselian elements. Breaking news at line 1586 already correctly references 'path of totality' / 'totality circle'. Only line 1754 (eg-falsifiability) is wrong, and it is exactly the paragraph the falsifiability module's fm-jump anchor scrolls readers to — so it has elevated traffic and is a credibility attack surface. The aria-label denominator collision (verdict-bars '22 of 71 claims' adjacent to module 'Status: 0 of 69 claims') is a genuine screen-reader confusion vector that the visible caption already disambiguates for sighted readers; matching the bar aria-label noun to the visible caption noun ('catalog entries') is the smallest fix.
proposal_review.proposal_patches_notes: P1 find/replace strings are exact and unique (single-occurrence). P2 find string is the verbatim template literal at generate-html.js line 489. P3 anchor (the EXP-271 sub-meter→sub-0.01% pair) is unique and immediately precedes the EXP-212 block — clean insert point.

integration_status.diagnosis: Phantom integration. The decider edited its clone at 2026-04-29T09:23, the push hit 403 (the recurring Devilwench credential issue), and the universal-pusher rescue path explicitly excludes build-scripts/* and test.js (these are in NEVER_PUSH because workspace-sync should not auto-push source code without operator review). The committed-but-unpushed source files therefore stayed only inside the decider's now-discarded clone. FUSE was never updated either — meaning the push-failure-block-to-FUSE behavior described in the universal-pusher CLAUDE.md note did not propagate source files to the workspace mount in this case. Net effect: tracker, closed-issues, and daily-report all assert EXP-277 is shipped, but no observable state in git, FUSE, or the rendered docs/index.html reflects any of the three patches. This is the same EXP-271 pattern operator-rescued as commit ac474a9 on 2026-04-29 06:28 (24h before EXP-277's phantom integration), and it is the FIFTH occurrence of this rescue pattern in 6 days per the operator-rescue commit message.

holes_found.description: PHANTOM INTEGRATION: ISS-1694/1695/1696/1717/1718 are recorded as fixed by EXP-277 ('fix_details: EXP-277 applied this run'), and expansion-tracker shows EXP-277 integrated:true, but P1, P2, and P3 are absent from build-scripts/generate-html.js, test.js, FUSE workspace, and docs/index.html. The most-trafficked consequence is that the eg-falsifiability paragraph (line 673 of docs/index.html — the literal landing target of the falsifiability module's fm-jump anchor) STILL says 'the August 12, 2026 annular solar eclipse', flatly contradicting our own Section 4.2.7 (line 2949) which correctly says 'magnitude of 1.0386 — total, not annular'. This is a self-contradiction within the published page that any reader who scrolls between sections can spot, and it sits on a page that explicitly invites that scrolling (the fm-jump CTA points to it).
holes_found.recommendation: Operator-rescue lift the three patches to git, exactly the EXP-271 procedure that landed in commit ac474a9. Source artifacts in the EXP-277.json patches[] array are exact_string_replace and insert_after_anchor with verifiable unique find strings — application is mechanical, no judgment calls. After lift, regenerate docs/index.html via node build.js html and commit. SEPARATELY: re-open ISS-1694/1695/1696/1717/1718 (or transition them to a 'needs-rescue' substatus distinct from 'fixed') so the closure record reflects observable git state rather than decider-clone state. Until landed, the tracker is asserting a false 'shipped' in five places.

holes_found.description: P3 negative-regex hole: the EXP-277 P3 assertion uses /aria-label="(?:Misleading|Refuted|Self-Contradicted|Standard Model Explains|Speculative|Outside-Model)[^"]*: \d+ of \d+ claims \(/ to assert the legacy noun 'claims' is gone. Three of those six verdict alternatives don't match any actual verdict-bar in docs/index.html: 'Standard Model Explains' (the actual verdict label is 'Std Model Explains' — exact-match required, no abbreviation collapse), 'Speculative' (not a verdict in this catalog), and 'Outside-Model' (not a verdict). Three real verdicts are missing from the alternation: 'Std Model Explains' (18/71), 'Unfalsifiable' (4/71), and 'Not Demonstrated' (4/71). After patches land, this regex would still pass against a regression where any of those three bars reverted to 'claims', because none of those verdict names is in the alternation. The positive assertion (every verdict bar says 'catalog entries') would catch that regression, but the explicit negative guard is incomplete — defeating the dual-assertion design.
holes_found.recommendation: Before applying, fix the P3 negative regex alternation to: (?:Misleading|Not Demonstrated|Refuted by Data|Self-Contradicted|Std Model Explains|Unfalsifiable). Drop 'Refuted'→'Refuted by Data' for full-name matching, drop the unused 'Standard Model Explains|Speculative|Outside-Model'. The Status row (the falsifiability module's '0 of 69 claims') intentionally retains 'claims' per EXP-277 design ('the 69 ARE dome-claimed wins'), so it must NOT be in the negative alternation — and it isn't, since 'Status' is not a verdict. That distinction is preserved.

holes_found.description: Audit-trail comment in EXP-277.holes_addressed cross-references EXP-256 c2 H1 line numbers as 'line 1754 of generate-html.js (line 673 of docs/index.html)' — this is correct as of the snapshot reviewed but is fragile to sections.json/build-scripts churn. If line 1754 shifts on a future generate-html.js edit (it has, in fact, shifted between the EXP-271 and EXP-276 integrations), the patch's exact-string match still works (find string is content-based) but the audit comment becomes misleading. Not a blocker — flagged for future patches to lean on stable IDs/anchors rather than line numbers.
holes_found.recommendation: Future patch packages should reference stable anchor IDs (#eg-falsifiability) rather than line numbers in their rationale text. Mechanical find/replace is unaffected.

stronger_arguments.description: The EXP-277 narrative undersells the credibility cost of the 'annular' error. Per the EXP-256 c2 H3 framing, this paragraph is what readers land on when they click the falsifiability module's fm-jump CTA — the module is the most-prominent Overview-tab CTA after the verdict-bars. A defender can use 'they don't even know what kind of eclipse it is' to discredit the entire falsifiability framework that the module advertises. The fact that we ALSO get it right at line 2949 makes it WORSE for the defender's narrative, not better — internal inconsistency on a basic factual point looks more careless than uniform error. Section 2.7's correct treatment is the audit-trail evidence that we know better; line 1754's 'annular' looks like a sloppy copy-paste from an earlier draft. The fix is one word — there is no excuse for it to remain unshipped 31 hours after being marked 'fixed'.

The decider's 'fix_details: EXP-277 applied this run: generate-html.js + test.js patches' assertion in five closed-issues records is itself a stronger version of the original ISS-1696 H1 complaint. ISS-1696 was about screen-reader denominator confusion; the deeper failure mode is that the audit trail (closed-issues + expansion-tracker + daily-report) records ARE the source of truth for 'is this fixed?', and they are now lying about EXP-277. A reviewer doing a coverage check ('have all EXP-256 holes been closed?') gets a confident YES from the tracker, but 'verify the actual page' returns NO. That divergence is structurally worse than the original 71/69 noun ambiguity ever was.

This is the FIFTH operator-rescue in 6 days for source-file integrations stuck behind the 403 push barrier (per ac474a9 commit message: 'ad08e61 (2026-04-26 docs build drift), 228df64 (EXP-254 SVGs), 5a29442 (EXP-255/256/257/261/264 generate-html), [ac474a9 EXP-271 falsifiability-module], EXP-277 [now]'). At this rate the 'rescue every few days' cadence is the actual integration latency for any source-file change — not a tail-event but the median path. The HNOTE-OPERATOR-DECIDER-EXPANSION-TRACKER-SYNC-001 amendment and a possible staging-branch pattern flagged in the ac474a9 commit message are the structural fix; EXP-277 is the latest data point showing the cost.

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP-277 proposal itself is well-engineered. It identifies the right two-of-seven holes that genuinely needed a code patch (annular factual error + missing exactly-once test) versus the five-of-seven already-resolved-by-EXP-271 (denominator language, 0/69 framing, visual order, WCAG, asymmetric framing); it provides a clean closure audit trail for the resolved-but-tracked items; it subsumes the duplicate EXP-280 with explicit subsumed_by metadata; the find/replace strings are exact and unique; the test additions are tagged 'EXP-277' for grep clarity; and the verdict-bar aria-label noun change is a one-character class fix that closes the screen-reader denominator gap without adding visible clutter to a page EXP-271 already disambiguated visually. The decider's confidence in marking it integrated reflects that proposal quality. The failure is in the SHIP path, not the decision.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Proposal quality and integration-state truthfulness are independent. A perfectly-engineered patch that never reaches origin/main is observably equivalent to no patch at all from the reader's perspective, and the tracker that asserts otherwise is now actively misleading any process (curmudgeon coverage scan, holistic review, operator audit) that trusts it. The next reviewer who reads closed-issues and sees ISS-1718 'fixed' will not re-flag the annular error — that's exactly what happened in this curmudgeon cycle: the queue item was pushed because the decider knew curmudgeon hadn't reviewed yet, but the linked closure records say the underlying defect is gone. Without operator rescue, EXP-277's positive content (the proposal) cannot offset its operational lie (the recorded integration).

recommended_action_detail: Two coordinated actions, both required: (1) OPERATOR-RESCUE LIFT — apply the three EXP-277.patches to build-scripts/generate-html.js and test.js (after the regex fix in hole 2), commit, regenerate docs/index.html, push. Same pattern as ac474a9. (2) RE-OPEN OR FLAG-NEEDS-RESCUE on ISS-1694/1695/1696/1717/1718 until step 1 lands; or alternately leave them closed but add a closure-correction record stating the patches were not in main at the time of closure. The decider should also amend its push-failure handling for source files: when push 403s and the changed files are in NEVER_PUSH, mark related issues as status:'fix-in-flight' rather than 'fixed' (or refuse to close them) until an operator rescue commit lands. Without that change, every source-file integration in the 403 environment will produce the same phantom-fixed pattern.

text_fingerprint.tests/run.sh status: 3513 passed, 0 failed (does not exercise EXP-277-specific assertions because they were never inserted)

deeper_analysis:
The Step 2b 'content-exists guard' in curmudgeon.md says 'For section rewrites referencing an EXP, check that the EXP has been integrated into sections.json. If the target content doesn't exist yet, skip this item.' EXP-277 is target_type:section-rewrite by queue convention but it does not edit sections.json — it edits generate-html.js and test.js. The integration target is the rendered build artifact, not a section blob. The strict reading of the guard would have me skip this item; the steel-man reading recognizes that 'integrated' here means 'visible at line 673 of docs/index.html', which it isn't. I chose to write the review because the failure mode (phantom integration) is precisely the thing curmudgeon coverage exists to catch — if I had skipped, the decider's tracker would continue asserting EXP-277 shipped indefinitely. The Step 5 staleness check (git pull --rebase) was performed and confirmed: the patches are still absent post-pull. Step 5's 'if all findings have been superseded' branch does not apply here — the entire finding IS that nothing has been superseded.

Counter-argument the operator should consider: this review's load-bearing claim is that the 'annular' factual error is publicly visible. Verify by hitting https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ and Cmd-F 'annular' in the Overview tab. If the rendered live page differs from docs/index.html in this clone, the GitHub Pages build pipeline may have recompiled from a different source, and the 'phantom' framing weakens. As of this clone (HEAD 0d7b151, post-pull 2026-04-30T~14:30Z), docs/index.html line 673 contains the 'annular' word and is the expected GitHub Pages output. The tests/run.sh shows 3513 passing, none of which reference EXP-277 — consistent with patches never inserted.

What I am NOT claiming: I am not claiming the decider acted in bad faith. The decider's clone-side experience is that it ran the patches, ran tests, and pushed; the 403 is the credential issue, not a decider bug. The fault sits in the 'we mark issues fixed when the decider's local clone is satisfied' policy interacting with the 'source files don't auto-push' NEVER_PUSH list. The fix is policy-level, not decider-bug-level.

summary_for_decider: EXP-277 is a phantom integration. Tracker says shipped, no observable state reflects shipment. Critical: 'annular' factual error remains live on docs/index.html line 673 (the falsifiability-module's fm-jump landing target). Recommendation: operator-rescue lift the patches per ac474a9 EXP-271 procedure, after fixing P3's negative-regex alternation (replace 'Standard Model Explains|Speculative|Outside-Model' with 'Std Model Explains|Unfalsifiable|Not Demonstrated|Refuted by Data'). Re-open or flag-needs-rescue on ISS-1694/1695/1696/1717/1718 until patches land. Structural recommendation: decider's push-failure handling for NEVER_PUSH source files should mark issues 'fix-in-flight' rather than 'fixed' — without that, every source-file integration in the 403 environment produces the same phantom-fixed pattern (5x in 6 days now).

defense_attempts.defense: The decider acted correctly given its local clone state; the 403 is environmental and not a decider-logic bug. Closing issues at decision-time rather than push-confirmation-time is faster and reduces tracker churn in the normal case.
defense_attempts.response: Granted in full for the decision-time logic. The cost of the speed optimization is that 5/5 source-file integrations in the past 6 days have been incorrectly marked 'fixed' until operator rescue. At that rate the false-positive cost dominates the latency benefit. The structural fix (mark fix-in-flight when push 403s and changed files are in NEVER_PUSH) is local to the push-failure block, not a redesign.

defense_attempts.defense: This is a duplicate of the EXP-271 rescue pattern; it will resolve when the operator next rescues. The curmudgeon doesn't need to flag it — operator already knows from the daily-report.
defense_attempts.response: The morning-briefing 14:05Z immediate items list ranks ISS-1742 (already-fixed) and ISS-1752 (4.2.7a MST math) above any mention of EXP-277. Without an explicit curmudgeon flag the EXP-277 lift may slip below the immediate-action waterline indefinitely while the tracker continues asserting 'fixed'. Recording the phantom pattern in a review file is the curmudgeon's lever for elevating it.
